reviews.review_description: I downloaded this game thinking it would amazing but then when I went to make the snowflake thing and I did everything it said and it wouldn’t sell then I uninstalled it and came a couple of weeks later and reinstalled it but then it didn’t give me another tutorial or anything.  The game is absolutely terrible.

developer_response.body:
Hello,

We're sorry to hear you had a negative experience with our game.

You can always reset the tutorial by following these steps:
1. From the Main Menu, open Settings
2. Tap "Reset Tutorial"

Try giving it another spin in case you missed something. If that doesn't work for you, feel free to email us at office@ingenious-studios.com with details and screenshots, and we'll do our best to help you out.

reviews.review_description: It looked awesome so I bought it but when I did I realized I can’t even play it because it has standing only and I’m disabled I can’t stand!! Fix that problem then I’ll be happy 1 star!!

developer_response.body:
Hi there,

We're sorry to hear you encountered a barrier in playing our game.

Our game does come with an accessibility option to adjust the player's height. It was designed to make the game playable while sitting, as well as by children and little people (we have an option for exceptionally tall people, as well).

From the main menu, open Settings and adjust the Height setting to low height. This will raise your eye level in-game and allow you to interact with all objects.

We hope this removes the barrier for you and thank you so much for your interest in our game! Please let us know whether this works, and if you encounter any further problems.

reviews.review_title: The best fantasy game I got into
reviews.review_description: this game is literally so much fun. This is like how I got in the fantasy literally, I’m not joking like you make the potion and you make weapons like how could that not be better but there is a couple things they could add maybe like the goblins could give you coins and then there’s like a button to go to like a shop or something and you can get more items and resources to make more stuff.￼ overall, it is a very fun game. I do love it. This is how I really got into fantasy stuff by this game right here. It is the best game I have ever played in my life. I I just wish I had ￼always knew about this game.￼

developer_response.body:
We're thrilled you love the game! Thank you so much for the kind words!

As for your feedback, we absolutely agree! The sequel will have the exact mechanic you're describing: customers pay you and you spend that money to upgrade your shop or buy cool fidgets to play around with :)

We're releasing it in Early Access this week, so you can check it out (though the money system itself isn't implemented yet).

reviews.review_description: Loving it already, even with just one level 4! I'm really hoping for some shop upgrades soon – more magic types and extra room to move around would be amazing. I'm a big fan of games where you can slowly expand your shop and, of course, any game with a sandbox mode for free creation! ✨🎮

developer_response.body: Hey whatup, thank you so much, that really means a lot to hear! And great news, as you progress through the Challenge mode, each completed level unlocks new recipes or expands and upgrades your shop, so there is definitely more to look forward to. Really appreciate you taking the time to leave a review. ♥

reviews.review_title: Best free game in the market
reviews.review_description: If I had to choose a favorite free vr game I would choose this, it's not perfect but that doesn't matter.

developer_response.body: Hey Error Cat! Thank you so much for the kind words and for your Support! It means a lot to the whole team to be picked as one of your favorites. ♥ Shopenkraft was free during Early Access, and because you joined us early, you received the full game as a free upgrade when we launched. We are really happy you’re enjoying it! ♥

reviews.review_description: I can't play game because my hands are stuck in the ground making me unable to start the game

developer_response.body: Hey Laxinmaxin, we are really sorry you ran into this issue. We understand how frustrating that can be and want to help get it sorted. In some cases, VR hands getting stuck in the ground turned out to be a controller or tracking issue. One thing that often fixes it is unpairing and then re-pairing the controllers (Meta support page: meta.com/help/quest/967070027432609). If that doesn't help, could you please share a short video with us and include steps you've already tried to office@ingenious-studios.com or join our Discord (discord.gg/nbd3vqZxEx) and we'll help figure it out together. 🙏

reviews.review_description:
Used one bottle cap and it did not respawn. Stuck on first level. Cannot find it anywhere. 

Edit to the edit: The devs really work to solve issues. The glitch is fixed and they personally emailed me to let me know. Updating my review because it is a great game. 

Edit: after replaying I know right when it occurs. On level one, second customer. Right when you go to make the sword, as soon as I put the metal in the forge, I watched the bottle cap vanish. It just disappeared. Definitely a glitch.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for updating your review! Awesome to see that you are enjoying the new update and that you are no longer experiencing any issues. If anything comes up, we are one message away! Happy Krafting!

reviews.review_description: It has it bug's like it's a bit hard to handle the hammer and the clamps Plus on the first level I couldn't figure out where the cork was so I had to restart and then it showed me that you had to pull it from the chain The ore keeps getting stuck behind the smelter and The drawers for the weapons are very hard to grab and keep getting stuck it'd be nice if there is a different mechanic to close the drawers after you grab the hot metal but besides the small issues it is a very fun game and has a lot of gameplay to offer is a solo player my new favorite free crafting game

developer_response.body: Hi Austin! Thanks for the feedback. Some parts can be a bit tricky the first time around, so it’s helpful to hear where you ran into issues. Glad you managed to figure it out. Really cool to hear you ended up enjoying it. Thank you for the kind words!

reviews.review_description: Soooooo fun I’ve been playing for hours and can’t stop definitely worth it play now

developer_response.body: Hey Rich! We are so happy to hear you're enjoying it! Appreciate you taking the time to leave a review, and hope you keep having fun with the game. ♥

reviews.review_description: I'm really diggin this game so far. its a ton of fun but it could use just a little more love when it comes to grabbing things and moving stuff around can be a lil janky at times but not enough to ruin my good time but it tries. other than that I'm loving it

developer_response.body: Hey Brojahfrey! Appreciate the kind words! We are happy to hear you are enjoying the game and glad some of the wobbly juggling didn't stop the fun for you! It is an occupational hazard of being a magical shopkeeper 😄 Thanks for the love and for sticking with it!

reviews.review_description: I enjoyed every second playing this game, it is very beautiful and fun to play.

developer_response.body: Hi Dooshan! Really glad to hear you had a good time with it. Thank you for the kind words!

reviews.review_description:
I like the game, but I can't even get past the
 tutorial level because the bottle cap disappears after the first customer, and onto that, the bottle cap doesn't appear when you close the potion I mean the game is fully broken for me and I've deleted it and re-download it and even restarted my headset and it still hasn't changed.

developer_response.body: UPDATE: The issue you reported with the corks disappearing has been fixed in our latest patch! 🎉 Thank you once again for taking the time to point this out to us, and we appreciate your patience while we worked on it. Let us know if you spot anything else, and Happy krafting!

reviews.review_description: Frustrating. Stuff falls you have to restart. So glitchy. Good concept cute like the graphics. No explanation on level two and after losing stuff cause it fell and no idea how to make the sword ends without hilts I'm done. Tutorial doesn't keep up. It gets stuck on things and then you're like great what now. So yeah if it gets fixed yes. As is no at all

developer_response.body: Hey there! Our latest update, the Community Patch, is packed with quality-of-life improvements, bug fixes, and a few shiny surprises! We’d love for you to try the game again and see if everything’s working smoothly now. Let us know if you spot anything else, and happy krafting!

reviews.review_description:
But..... This game at 3.9 is ridiculous.  5 star game, worth the money spent.  I love it.  As far as the cork issue that I read in a previous review... Sometimes things fall or you drop something.  Use your magic hand trigger to pick it up again.  It can be hard to see because it is a small item.  

Anyways, boosting the rating.  If you enjoy fantasy and magic, it is great immersion.  Solid crafting game, and as you progress you unlock more recipes, reagents and ingredients.

developer_response.body: Wow, thank you so much for the amazing review and for sharing your thoughts about Shopenkraft! We are so glad to hear that you are enjoying the game, and we really appreciate you sharing some helpful tips for other players. Your support means the world to us. Happy Krafting!

reviews.review_description: Pretty fun game since you can create potions and weapons I wish there were even more potions and stuff to make and also wish the whole area was a lot bigger and you could customize the place but overall w game

developer_response.body: Hey Jacky! We are so glad to hear you're having fun! As you level up, you will unlock even more recipes - including new potions, weapons, and trinkets - so there is plenty of more crafting ahead! You will also notice your shop changing as you progress - passing certain levels unlocks shop decorations, so by the time you master the game, your shop will be fully decked out in all its glory! We don't want to spoil too much, but later levels bring some seriously complex crafting (especially in challenge mode). We appreciate your feedback on the play area size and customization. Thanks for playing, and Happy Krafting!

reviews.review_description: I've looked for a crafting or cooking game where I could do it as a challenge and or just chill back no timers or rushes just kraft and this game does has multiple settings just for that. Very smooth expierence, I work in a kitchen and I got that same zen flow feel (plus telekenisis) on a good day when I got used to things which happened very quickly. Thier mechanics are enjoyable things are where I leave them unless I use a reset lever (thanks for that devs) which really adds to the flow. Only encountered 3 bugs one when resetting position using the controller it put me outside but doing it again put me right back where I needed to be so no biggie there took an extra 2 seconds to fix. The other is current and seems to just be from impact points but when I grab the axe tray it won't grab half the time or when it does it wont slide out same with pushing it back in but the easy way around that is to grab it underhand when I go for it and hasn't bugged yet good chance its my height working against me on that one. I did have a stand alone glitch where the handle to the drawer went back in but pulling the reset lever popped it back out. I did not know i owned the game which is awesome and my whole family from 6 to 35 have enjoyed it. I only knew i owned it when I went to buy it, at 15$ I recommended it to friends easily.

developer_response.body: Wow, thank you so much for the awesome review! It’s really great to hear that Shopenkraft is giving you that zen vibe you were hoping for. We are glad the tool reset lever’s been useful, and appreciate you mentioning the bugs you encountered. We are definitely looking into those to make sure everything runs smoother. It’s also amazing that your whole family is enjoying the game! Thank you for sharing it with them and recommending it to friends. Your support means a lot to us. Happy Krafting!

reviews.review_description:
It’s a fun game it gets a little repetitive but over all it’s fun. There’s some bugs and I wish when you get items the shop will look nicer.
 ￼￼

developer_response.body:
Hey there! We are glad to hear you're having fun! We get that some parts can feel a bit repetitive. As you progress, you'll unlock new recipes, levels, and shop decorations - so there's always something new to craft and discover! Your shop will gradually transform as you level up, unlocking new decorations along the way.
If you’ve run into any bugs, could you please write us with more details regarding the issue you experienced (what exactly you did, how exactly the problem manifests, etc.) at office@ingenious-studios.com? Alternatively, you can join our discord server as we have a dedicated bug report section - https://discord.gg/nbd3vqZxEx

reviews.review_description: I can't wait for more updates and the fact that the game is In early access is even better  great shopen!

developer_response.body: Hey there! We're so glad you're loving the game! Thanks for taking the time to share your excitement—it means the world to us. Happy Krafting!

reviews.review_description:
It’s a solid fantasy shop VR game. The￼ only thing I’d say that needs improving is mainly quality of life stuff. 

Like items that fall in hard to reach places being able to teleport back to their spot. Or making it easier to keep track of your tools, maybe letting the player swap between them.

The game is still early release so there’s definitely time for them to improve small things like these.

developer_response.body: Hey Pigwitz! Thank you so much for your kind words. We are happy to hear that you enjoyed playing Shopenkraft in Early Access. For the full release, we will have a distance grabber, and this will help out with materials or crafted items falling in places. We also have a tool reset lever, right next to the hammer, and it returns all of the shop tools to their original location. We have a lot of improvements lined up for the full release and we can't wait to share it with you! Thanks again for your support!

reviews.review_title: I got mine when it was freee
reviews.review_description: So happy I didn’t have to pay

developer_response.body: Hey Wicked! Glad you got a chance to grab it during Early Access! We appreciate your continued support and hope you're enjoying the game. Happy Krafting!

reviews.review_description: The Meta Quest pro does not work with this app, even though it indicates that Meta quest pro is a supported platform. Please remove that from thevsupported platform list because I paid for this app and cannot use it at all.

developer_response.body: Hey there Toiah, we sincerely apologize for the inconvenience! We completely understand how frustrating it can be when a game isn't working on your device as expected. We are currently working closely with Meta to address this issue and remove the Quest Pro from our supported devices list. We appreciate your patience as we work on resolving this. In the meantime, we recommend reaching out to Meta for a refund if you're unable to use the app. Thank you for your understanding!

reviews.review_description: It is sooooo good because you can feel magic in real life finally

developer_response.body: Hey Corey! Thank you so much for trying out our game and sharing your thoughts. It's awesome to hear that you felt the magic vibe as this is exactly what we are going for. We can't wait to show you what we have prepared for the full release!

reviews.review_title: I like it but a problem
reviews.review_description: It's a good game just the axe won't work also can you make it multiplayer or free

developer_response.body: Hey Moe, thank you for sharing your thoughts about Shopenkraft! We are happy to hear you liked playing the game. It turns out that a last-minute fix we pushed broke the axe forging right before our full version release, which is disastrous! The issue is now fixed, so you should be able to proceed as normal. As for multiplayer, it’s something we’d love to explore in the future, but for now, we’re focusing on delivering the best experience in the current game. We really appreciate your feedback and hope you continue to enjoy playing!

reviews.review_description: Good game but sometimes the metal would get stuck behind the anvil

developer_response.body: Hey Safflad, thanks so much for taking the time to share your thoughts on the full release! We’re really glad to hear you’re enjoying the game overall. Sorry to hear about the metal getting stuck behind the anvil—that’s definitely not how it’s supposed to work. If that happens again, you can use the distance grabber feature to grab objects that are tough to reach. Hope that helps, and thanks again for your feedback!

reviews.review_description: Idk

developer_response.body: Hi Nico, thanks for the review! Glad you had a good time with the Early Access version. Just a heads-up, the full version is out now with a ton of new stuff to check out—more recipes, features, and challenges. Would love to hear what you think if you give it a spin. Appreciate you playing!

reviews.review_title: Played for the first time - no tutorial!
reviews.review_description: Very frustrating to play and not have a clue what to do, The first time playing, no tutorial was visible unless the tutorial is where the ingame characters walk up to the desk and ask for things.  :(

developer_response.body:
Hey Mark! First, we'd like to thank you for trying out Shopenkraft's Magic Goods!

We are sorry to hear that while playing, you encountered issues with the in-game tutorial. Currently, the game tutorial is designed to show visual hints to guide you, but we understand that it can sometimes be easy to miss. For the full release, we'll also add extra effects, like guiding particles, to make sure that every new Shopkeeper knows exactly where to go.

For now, try looking around the shop, the first tutorial icon should pop up to your right, above the ore dispenser. Whenever you are feeling stuck, look around the shop and you'll find similar indicators on what to do next. 

We hope you'll give Shopenkraft another try as we continue to work on improvements!

reviews.review_description:
It was great but I too still had a problem with the axe drawer even the latest version, also not only did it not close sometimes it telephoned onto the smelter and in the middle of the shop.

Lastly sometimes when I use tools (specifically the hammer) they either flew out of my hand or in some cases or just dropped  out of my hands (and no this wasn't me being clumsy)

But overall this was really great and amazing, can't wait for the full release.

developer_response.body: Hey Leroy, thanks for the detailed review—it means a lot! We are sorry to hear about the axe drawer and hammer issues. Those bugs definitely shouldn’t be happening, but we’ve worked on squashing a lot of them in the full version! Really appreciate you sticking with us despite the hiccups, and we’re happy to hear you’re enjoying the game overall. Hope the full release blows you away!

reviews.review_description:
OK so I've only played this just to see what it was like before my daughter tried it...
Now I'm an hour into playing and didn't realise how long I was playing.

It's a very fun game and I will be playing it more 👍

I did find a possible bug when you use the quest button to re-center your position you get moved to behind the forge

Other than that issue great job love it.

Update : bugs all fixed and and thanks to the devs for the giving the early access players the full game 😄

developer_response.body: Hey Ablebearmaker, thank you so much for the awesome review! We are so happy you loved playing Shopenkraft and how it pulled you in even before your daughter got her turn! We're also glad to know the issues you encountered have been resolved and that you're loving the full experience. Happy Krafting, and thank you for your support!

reviews.review_description:
This game is very promising in its early access. The mechanics are fun (always loved smithing in VR, ever since playing a Township Tale), potion brewing, etc. Keep it up!

But one thing I want to mention about this game is the music. They sound great, don’t get me wrong, but after a while, I’ve noticed that in some song, they had some “graininess” to them, which led me to believe that some music was done by AI. Again, I’m not judging, it’s a great way to do placeholder music, but I can’t wait to what the composers (if any) will brew some good stuff up!

developer_response.body: Hello Alex! Thank you so much for trying out Shopenkraft’s Magic Goods and for taking the time to share your thoughts. We are happy to hear you’re enjoying the smithing and potion-brewing mechanics! As for the music, you’re spot on—it is AI-generated. Being a smaller studio, we’ve had to make some tough choices with our budget, and for this game, we decided this was the best option to bring our vision to life. We appreciate your understanding and we are excited that you see the potential in what we’re creating. We can’t wait for you to experience everything we’ve got lined up for the full release!

reviews.review_title: Good game But it does have some problems
reviews.review_description: So first of all this really remindes me about a game called dungeon brew master. I’ve always wanted to get that game but it’s on steam so I can’t :( but this game is its distant cousin… just more laggy. Now with the issues 1 the hands are weird: so when I would make a weapon and I go to the drawers I’d try to grab them but nope! I grab on to a totally different thing or I’d just not grab it at all! 2very repetitive: after you do some levels it seemed like you did all.3items go over the counter: it’s pretty self explanatory,you let go of a item over the counter, oops! You’re never seeing that again! Oh well! 4 recentering is bad: so if a can’t grab some thing over the counter I try to go over my boundary but then it automatically resets it to a place I don’t want it to be! So I recenter it then… oh! It’s not alined oh well guessing have to restart the game oh well! Please fix these problems

developer_response.body: Hello Julian! Thanks so much for checking out Shopenkraft’s Magic Goods! We’re glad you gave it a go and appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts—it means a lot to us! This is the Early Access version, so it includes the first 10 levels and one game mode. The full release will bring 30+ levels, four game modes, and many improvements to keep things fresh and exciting. We’re aware of the issues you mentioned. The grabbing mechanics and drawer interactions will be much smoother in the full release. Items disappearing over the counter is intentional to avoid clutter and keep optimal performance, but don’t worry—ingredients will respawn, and tools can be reset using the lever near the hammer. We’re also working on better recentering and plan to add a height adjustment option for a more seamless experience. Thank you once again for your support and we hope you'll enjoy the full release even more!

reviews.review_title: Not Sure About It Just Yet..
reviews.review_description:
The game looks fine, and even if it took me a good minute to figure out exactly what the tutorial prompts were trying to tell me at times, all of the mechanics seem basically as functional as other crafting/ food prep VR games I've played.
One thing I hate, and the biggest/ only reason I never play and requested refund on 1 or 2 other crafting titles; you have no virtual locomotion. No way to even interact with the freakin' menu without walking up to it and physically pressing the necessary buttons...
It's a small space, sure, but not so small that you aren't taking steps in every which direction needed. Mixed with the frustrating forced Snap turning (my god, why do some devs STILL do this as the ONLY option?..), I find myself physically turning at times as well.None of this works for very long in my VERY limited space, so I'm needing to recalibrate way more frequently than I'd like whilst making sure I'm not knocking over/ hitting anything and being lined up correctly in-game at the same time...

Thankfully there's no time limit as with others outside of specific game modes, but it still doesn't make for a smooth gameplay experience for me, all because very simple things like locomotion (yes, it would be limited, but still) and an option for smooth turning...


I'll likely update my score and review after playing a bit more, but a very mid(though not bad) first impression isn't quite what I was hoping for before purchasing.

developer_response.body:
Hi there James! Thank you so much for taking the time to share your thoughts about Shopenkraft.

Our focus was to fully immerse players in the hands-on crafting experience, which is why there is no virtual locomotion and all movement and interactions are physical. We understand how limited play space can be a challenge, and we appreciate your input on how that impacts your gameplay.  

One thing that might help is the distance grabber feature. To use it, press the grip trigger, after which the effect should become visible, then point to the object you wish to grab, and then press the back trigger. It takes some time to get used to, but hopefully it helps out. 

We also appreciate your input on smooth turning. We are always listening to our player feedback, and while we currently don't have any updates on additional comfort options, if there are any changes in the future, we will be sure to keep you informed.

We hope that as you spend more time in the game, it will start to feel more natural for you, and if you have any additional thoughts or suggestions, let us know! Happy Krafting!

reviews.review_description: I just got this game but on my third customer I couldn’t get the cork to the potion it wouldn’t appear I restarted the headset and everything! It dosnt work!

developer_response.body:
Hi Angma,

Thanks for trying out the game! It's strange that the cork isn't appearing for your third customer if it appeared successfully for the first. A new cork should spawn at the end of the chain immediately after you pull the previous one off with your hand.
The only time it won't respawn is if you've already picked 5 corks from the chain and they're all lying around your shop unused (the game will never spawn more than 5 corks at once).

Could you write us with more details regarding your issue (what exactly you did, how exactly the problem manifests etc.) at office@ingenious-studios.com?

reviews.review_description: This game is so much fun. I had a blast playing it everything from the model designs to the fidgets to the gadgets to the music in the background and all for free game I recommend playing this for sure. Of course there are a few bugs cause the game is brand new￼

developer_response.body: Hey Johnny, thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts! We are so glad to hear you had a blast playing Shopenkraft in Early Access. Now that the full release is out, we have worked on addressing the bugs from the Early Access version. If you decide to give it another go, we’d love to hear what you think! Thanks for recommending the game—it truly means a lot to us!

reviews.review_description: I got this game only a couple hours before full release and the new full release is lovely! I think its a great game but I'm stuck on the last order of level two as when I have my hot axe    blade grab it with my tongs and set it on the anvil the spots to hammer don't pop up. I tried hammering in every nook and cranny but to no avail. I tried restarting the game and my headset but nothing seems to work. Also something else make it so you can go back to your last order not level!

developer_response.body:
Hi legendairy09,

Thank you so much for your report. It turns out that a last-minute fix we pushed broke the axe forging right before our full version release, which is disastrous! The issue is now fixed, although you will unfortunately lose your level progress, which means you'll have to replay level 1 as well. We're sorry for the inconvenience and hope you'll give the game another spin! It really opens up after level 3!

Thanks again for the report!

reviews.review_description:
Throughout the whole time I've been playing it's been really good except 3 problems 

The first problem is when I accidentally toss stuff over the counter I can't get it back if it's something I need to make stuff I have to restart the whole game if it's something I need to make I have to remake it. 


The second problem is the thing that you use to select the weapon is really bugged out especially the orcs the orcs one I somehow got sideways in the wall and they don't really come out well 

The third problem is when I'm handling the weapons the hammer falls out of my hands or the item we use to hold the metal steel drops that steal all sudden in the hammer glitches everywhere

developer_response.body:
Hello dogpool!

Thank you so much for trying out Shopenkraft's Magic Goods and for sharing your thoughts with us. We're happy to hear that you’ve been enjoying your time as a Shopkeeper!

Regarding the items tossed over the counter, they are designed to disappear after a short time to prevent clutter and maintain optimal performance. Don’t worry, though—all ingredients will respawn, so you’ll be able to retrieve them again. For tools, there’s a lever on the right-hand side of the shop (next to the hammer) that resets all tools to their original positions—this should save you from needing to restart the game!

We’ve also received feedback about the weapon drawers, and we’re happy to share that these issues have been resolved. In the latest version, the drawers work smoothly and as intended for a better experience.

As for the third issue, what you’ve described sounds like a collision problem, which we’ve been actively addressing.

We truly appreciate your feedback—it helps us fine-tune the game as we approach its full release. Thank you for being part of this journey, and we hope you’ll join us when Shopenkraft's Magic Goods is fully released!

reviews.review_title: Really fun but a some small issues
reviews.review_description: This game is so fun and I absolutely love it, but Sometimes I teleport out of the area I’m supposed to be and I can’t get back. Usually have to restart the whole game which is kinda annoying.

developer_response.body: Hi Liam, Thank you for the review—glad you’re having fun with the game! We are sorry to hear about the teleporting issue, that sounds super frustrating. Just so you know, you can reset your view by holding the Meta button on your controller, which might help avoid restarting the whole game. We’ve worked on ironing out bugs like this in the full release, so hopefully, things will feel smoother if you check it out. Thanks again for playing, and let us know if you run into anything else!

reviews.review_description: The early access is great! A bit short but it's a early acces so it's fine. I will keep my eyes on this game for the future. It's a nice game to just chill. I think it would be a nice game to play while siting on a desk chair or something that can turn on itself. It's great for VR beginners and I can see the difficulty that future combinations of ingredients will bring, and I LOVE it!! I recommend.

developer_response.body: Hello Tristan! We’re so happy to hear you enjoyed Shopenkraft’s Magic Goods! Thanks for giving it a try and sharing your thoughts—it’s awesome to hear you see its potential. We can’t wait to bring you even more fun combinations and content in the full release. Thanks for the recommendation—it means the world to us!

reviews.review_description: Honoestly, love the graphics and the style of play. Very relaxing, not challenging. However decently glad I got it!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for sharing your thoughts about Shopenkraft! We’re happy to hear you’re enjoying the graphics and the relaxing vibe. If you ever have any suggestions, feel free to drop by our Discord: https://discord.gg/nbd3vqZxEx. Happy Krafting!

reviews.review_title: Be the shop keeper in a d&d campaign
reviews.review_description: Honestly the most fun I've had in a vr game. It really gets your multitasking skills going. As long as you read what the customer wants you're fine. Great game and can't wait to play more. 😁

developer_response.body: Hi there, Lisa! Thank you so much for the amazing review. Hearing that this is the most fun you’ve had in a VR game truly makes our day. It gets even wilder as you progress, and we can’t wait for you to dive into the later stages of the game and become the ultimate master crafter along the way. Happy Krafting!

reviews.review_description:
I have a problem with the height I know I can change the size of the counter to fit my heights but it’s still too big for me so much so I hit my fan sometimes ￼￼￼because I have to reach up to get the items ￼I need
But overall, I absolutely love the game it makes my ADHD ￼give a lot of dopamine ￼to my brain ￼

developer_response.body: Hey there! Thanks for sharing your experience. Glad to hear you're enjoying being a Shopkeeper! For the height issue you mentioned, aside from adjusting the in-game settings, you can also modify your VR guardian or play space height - that could help bring things down a bit more. Hopefully, that does the trick! Happy Krafting!
